differentialBuckets.label: Addison's Disease: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

source.source_section: Addison's Disease generated endocrine workup: diagnostic frame
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL.

source.source_section: Addison's Disease generated endocrine workup: thresholds
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

differentialBuckets.action: Use local assay and patient context; do not apply numeric anchors without clinical interpretation.

redFlags.label: Adrenal crisis, severe hyperkalemia, shock, infection.

source.source_section: Addison's Disease generated endocrine workup: red flags
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

redFlags.action: Escalate urgently, reassess severity, and evaluate for dangerous mimics or complications.

requiredQuestions.label: Salt craving, hyperpigmentation, autoimmune history, weight loss/GI symptoms, orthostasis.

requiredExam.label: Orthostatic vitals, hyperpigmentation including oral mucosa/palmar creases, volume status.

requiredExam.action: Perform and document positive, negative, and unable-to-assess findings.

initialTests.label: 8 AM cortisol/ACTH.

source.source_section: Addison's Disease generated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L.

initialTests.label: Cosyntropin stimulation.

dispositionRules.action: High ACTH plus low cortisol confirms primary pattern; mineralocorticoid deficiency changes therapy to fludrocortisone/salt guidance.

reference_values: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent.
Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL.
Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L.
